rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by adding content only within the intended owner scope and without changing unrelated structural elements.
Use component type scope for feature-level contracts and implementation scope only for implementation-owned content such as bindings or assignments.
Ensure any inserted content references only names visible in that owner scope; avoid subcomponent paths outside their valid scope.
Maintain existing subcomponents, connections, properties, and ordering context when augmenting an implementation.
generic_rules: Respect owner/scope boundaries between component types and implementations.
Keep fusion minimal and additive so existing model structure and semantics are preserved.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Implementation-level additions belong after relevant subcomponents and connections when scope depends on them.
Do not introduce references to non-exposed properties or identifiers that are not structurally visible in the selected owner.
anti_patterns: Do not move feature-level logic into an implementation scope when it belongs to the component type interface.
Do not alter unrelated architecture, rename existing elements, or rewire connections during insertion.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE expression syntax details such as temporal recurrence and boolean operator forms
Validator-specific type-repair rules for AGREE declarations
Requirement threshold semantics beyond owner/scope preservation
